summary: iOS | Intermittent | DFU/Language Change fails at >90%
scenario: DFU or Language Change fails at >90% completion, causing firmware version to revert after restart or language to revert after brief use
description_clean: Intermittent DFU (Device Firmware Update) or Language Change fails at >90% completion. After a failed DFU, the device shows as up-to-date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. After a failed Language Change, the device may briefly use the new language then restart and revert to the previous language. This issue is intermittent and does not occur every time.
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android device.
2. Navigate to Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth audio device is connected.
3. Go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Firmware Update.
4. Initiate a Device Firmware Update (DFU) and allow it to progress past 90% completion.
5. Observe the DFU process; note if it fails at >90% completion.
6. After the failed DFU, check the firmware version displayed in Device Settings → [Device Name] → Firmware Update (should show as up-to-date).
7. Restart the Poly Bluetooth audio device (Power off/on).
8. Reconnect the device and check the firmware version again in Device Settings → [Device Name] → Firmware Update.
9. Alternatively, navigate to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Language.
10. Initiate a Language Change and allow it to progress past 90% completion.
11. Observe if the device briefly switches to the new language after the change.
12. Restart the Poly Bluetooth audio device (Power off/on).
13. Reconnect the device and check the language setting in Device Settings → [Device Name] → Language.
14. Repeat steps 3–13 multiple times to confirm the issue is intermittent.
expected: DFU completes successfully past 90% and the device retains the updated firmware after restart. Language Change completes successfully and the device retains the new language after restart.
actual: Intermittently, DFU fails at >90% completion. After failure, the device shows as up-to-date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. Language Change may briefly apply the new language but reverts to the previous language after restart.
